intent: task manager with user auth, CRUD tasks with title/description/status/due date, user isolation, filter by status, sort by due date

stories.title: New User Registers and Creates Their First Task
stories.narrative: A first-time user discovers the app, registers an account, creates their first task with a due date, and confirms it appears in their task list.

steps.action: register a new account with a unique email and password
steps.verify: registration succeeds and the user is authenticated
steps.verify_in_browser: registration form submits, user lands on the main task page or dashboard

steps.action: create a task with title 'Buy groceries', description 'Milk, eggs, bread', status 'pending', and due date set to tomorrow
steps.verify: task is created successfully with all fields stored correctly
steps.verify_in_browser: task appears in the list showing title, description, status, and due date

steps.action: view the full task list
steps.verify: exactly one task exists with title 'Buy groceries', correct description, status 'pending', and correct due date
steps.verify_in_browser: task list displays the task with all details visible

stories.title: Full Task Lifecycle: Create, Update, Complete, Delete
stories.narrative: A returning user logs in, creates a task, edits its title and description, changes its status to completed, verifies the changes, then deletes it and confirms it's gone.

steps.action: log in with existing credentials
steps.verify: login succeeds and user is authenticated
steps.verify_in_browser: user is redirected to the main page showing their tasks

steps.action: create a task with title 'Draft report', description 'Q1 financial summary', status 'pending', and due date one week from now
steps.verify: task is created with all fields stored correctly
steps.verify_in_browser: new task appears in the task list

steps.action: update the task created in step 2: change title to 'Draft Q1 report' and description to 'Q1 financial summary with charts'
steps.verify: task now has the updated title and description, other fields unchanged
steps.verify_in_browser: task list reflects the updated title and description

steps.action: change the status of the task from step 2 to 'completed'
steps.verify: task status is now 'completed', all other fields remain correct
steps.verify_in_browser: task shows as completed in the UI (checkmark, strikethrough, or status badge)

steps.action: delete the task from step 2
steps.verify: task no longer exists in the system
steps.verify_in_browser: task disappears from the task list

steps.action: view the full task list
steps.verify: the deleted task does not appear in the list
steps.verify_in_browser: task list does not contain 'Draft Q1 report'

stories.title: Two Users Cannot See Each Other's Tasks
stories.narrative: Two different users each create tasks. Each user can only see their own tasks, never the other's. Tests that user isolation is enforced at the data layer.

steps.action: register user A with a unique email and password
steps.verify: user A is registered and authenticated
steps.verify_in_browser: user A lands on the main page

steps.action: as user A, create a task with title 'Alice private task' and description 'Only Alice should see this'
steps.verify: task is created successfully for user A
steps.verify_in_browser: task appears in user A's task list

steps.action: log out user A
steps.verify: user A is logged out
steps.verify_in_browser: user is redirected to login or landing page

steps.action: register user B with a different unique email and password
steps.verify: user B is registered and authenticated
steps.verify_in_browser: user B lands on the main page

steps.action: as user B, create a task with title 'Bob private task' and description 'Only Bob should see this'
steps.verify: task is created successfully for user B
steps.verify_in_browser: task appears in user B's task list

steps.action: as user B, list all tasks
steps.verify: only 'Bob private task' appears; 'Alice private task' does NOT appear
steps.verify_in_browser: task list shows only Bob's task

steps.action: as user B, attempt to access user A's task directly by guessing its identifier
steps.verify: access is denied or task is not found — user B cannot read user A's task
steps.verify_in_browser: error message or not-found page is shown

steps.action: log out user B, log in as user A, and list all tasks
steps.verify: only 'Alice private task' appears; 'Bob private task' does NOT appear
steps.verify_in_browser: task list shows only Alice's task

stories.title: Tasks Persist After Logout and Login
stories.narrative: A user creates several tasks, logs out completely, logs back in, and verifies all tasks are still present with correct data. Tests data durability.

steps.action: register a new user with unique email and password
steps.verify: user is registered and authenticated
steps.verify_in_browser: user is on the main page

steps.action: create a task with title 'Persistent task 1', description 'Should survive logout', status 'pending', and due date three days from now
steps.verify: task is created successfully
steps.verify_in_browser: task appears in the list

steps.action: create a second task with title 'Persistent task 2', description 'Also survives logout', status 'completed', and due date one day from now
steps.verify: task is created successfully
steps.verify_in_browser: both tasks appear in the list

steps.action: log out completely
steps.verify: user session is terminated
steps.verify_in_browser: user is on the login or landing page, cannot access tasks

steps.action: log back in with the same credentials
steps.verify: login succeeds
steps.verify_in_browser: user is back on the main page

steps.action: list all tasks
steps.verify: both 'Persistent task 1' (pending, due in 3 days) and 'Persistent task 2' (completed, due in 1 day) are present with all original field values intact
steps.verify_in_browser: both tasks appear in the list with correct titles, descriptions, statuses, and due dates

stories.title: Unauthenticated Visitor Cannot Access Tasks
stories.narrative: An unauthenticated visitor tries to view, create, update, and delete tasks without logging in. All operations should be rejected.

steps.action: without logging in, attempt to list all tasks
steps.verify: request is rejected with an authentication error
steps.verify_in_browser: user is redirected to login page or shown an auth error

steps.action: without logging in, attempt to create a task with title 'Sneaky task'
steps.verify: request is rejected with an authentication error, no task is created
steps.verify_in_browser: form submission fails or user is redirected to login

steps.action: without logging in, attempt to access a task detail page by navigating directly to a task URL
steps.verify: request is rejected with an authentication error
steps.verify_in_browser: user is redirected to login page or shown an auth error

steps.action: without logging in, attempt to delete a task by guessing an identifier
steps.verify: request is rejected with an authentication error, no task is deleted
steps.verify_in_browser: operation fails, user is prompted to log in

stories.title: Filter Tasks by Status and Sort by Due Date
stories.narrative: A user creates multiple tasks with different statuses and due dates, then filters by status and sorts by due date to verify correct results.

steps.action: log in with existing credentials (or register a new user)
steps.verify: user is authenticated
steps.verify_in_browser: user is on the main page

steps.action: create a task with title 'Task A', status 'pending', and due date 5 days from now
steps.verify: task is created
steps.verify_in_browser: task appears in list

steps.action: create a task with title 'Task B', status 'completed', and due date 1 day from now
steps.verify: task is created
steps.verify_in_browser: task appears in list

steps.action: create a task with title 'Task C', status 'pending', and due date 2 days from now
steps.verify: task is created
steps.verify_in_browser: task appears in list

steps.action: create a task with title 'Task D', status 'in_progress', and due date 10 days from now
steps.verify: task is created
steps.verify_in_browser: task appears in list

steps.action: filter tasks by status 'pending'
steps.verify: only 'Task A' and 'Task C' are returned; 'Task B' and 'Task D' are excluded
steps.verify_in_browser: task list shows only pending tasks

steps.action: filter tasks by status 'completed'
steps.verify: only 'Task B' is returned
steps.verify_in_browser: task list shows only the completed task

steps.action: view all tasks sorted by due date ascending
steps.verify: tasks appear in order: Task B (1 day), Task C (2 days), Task A (5 days), Task D (10 days)
steps.verify_in_browser: task list displays tasks ordered by nearest due date first

stories.title: Edge Cases: Empty Fields, Long Strings, Special Characters
stories.narrative: A user tests the boundaries of task creation by submitting empty required fields, extremely long titles, and special characters including emoji and HTML tags.

steps.action: log in with existing credentials (or register a new user)
steps.verify: user is authenticated
steps.verify_in_browser: user is on the main page

steps.action: attempt to create a task with an empty title and no description
steps.verify: creation is rejected with a validation error — tasks require at least a title
steps.verify_in_browser: form shows a validation error message near the title field

steps.action: create a task with a very long title (1000+ characters of repeated text) and a normal description
steps.verify: either the task is created with the full title preserved, or a clear validation error is returned — no silent truncation or server error
steps.verify_in_browser: the long title is displayed correctly or a clear error message is shown

steps.action: create a task with title containing special characters: emoji (🎯📋), unicode (café, naïve), and HTML tags (<script>alert('xss')</script>)
steps.verify: task is created with special characters preserved correctly; HTML tags are escaped or stored safely — no XSS vulnerability
steps.verify_in_browser: title displays the emoji and unicode correctly; HTML tags are shown as text, not executed as code

steps.action: rapidly submit the same task creation form twice in quick succession (double-click the submit button)
steps.verify: either only one task is created, or if two are created, both are valid and retrievable — no server error or data corruption
steps.verify_in_browser: the task list does not show duplicates (or shows exactly two if the system allows it), with no error state
